title: Code modification using a virtual assistant
abstract: A system can receive an input via a chatbot interface. The input can be indicative of an update to a control parameter. The system can generate, using a large language model, a search query based on the input. The system can filter, using the search query, data related to control parameters. The system can detect, using the large language model, updates to the control parameters based on the filtered data. The system can identify, based on the updates to the control parameters and product metadata, products that fail to comply with the updates. The system can determine code segments within a source code corresponding to the products. The code segments can be related to the control parameters. The system can modify the code segments to comply with the updates to the control parameters. The system can execute the modified code segments to perform an operation.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system, comprising: one or more processors, coupled with memory, to: receive, from a client device, a textual input via a chatbot interface, the textual input indicative of an update to a control parameter; generate, using a large language model, a search query based on the textual input; filter, using the search query, data related to control parameters stored in a database; detect, using the large language model, updates to the control parameters based on the filtered data; identify, based on the updates to the control parameters and product metadata, one or more products that fail to comply with the updates; determine code segments within a source code corresponding to the one or more products, the code segments related to the control parameters; modify the code segments to comply with the updates to the control parameters; and execute the modified code segments to perform an operation. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the control parameters correspond to a set of rules defining operational standards for the one or more products. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are further configured to: input the textual input into the large language model to cause the large language model to classify the textual input into one or more search terms; and generate, using the large language model, the search query based on the one or more search terms. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the large language model is trained on a dataset comprising at least one of regulatory requirements, industry standards, or legislative directives. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are further configured to: identify, responsive to comparing the product metadata of the one or more products to compliance thresholds defined by the updates to the control parameters, that the one or more products fail to comply with the updates to the control parameters. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the product metadata includes information related to product configurations, operational settings, usage patterns, or deployment environments. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are further configured to: determine the code segments within the source code responsive to mapping the control parameters to the code segments implementing functionalities related to the control parameters. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are further configured to: execute the large language model, trained on a dataset of control parameters, to determine the code segments within the source code. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are further configured to: generate code modifications to the code segments based on the updates to the control parameters; and execute the generated code modifications to comply with the updates to the control parameters. 10. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are further configured to: input the modified code segments and the updates to the control parameters into the large language model to cause the large language model to generate a data structure; and 

 parse the data structure to display, via the client device, an electronic report indicating compliance of the one or more products with the updates to the control parameters. 11. A method, comprising: generating, using a large language model, a search query based on an input received via a chatbot interface indicative of an update to a control parameter; filtering, using the search query, data related to control parameters stored in a database; detecting, using the large language model, updates to the control parameters based on the filtered data; identifying, based on the updates to the control parameters and product metadata, one or more products that fail to comply with the updates; determining code segments within a source code corresponding to the one or more products, the code segments related to the control parameters; modifying the code segments to comply with the updates to the control parameters; and executing the modified code segments to perform an operation. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the control parameters correspond to a set of rules defining operational standards for the one or more products. 13. The method of claim 11, further comprising: inputting the input into the large language model to cause the large language model to classify the input into one or more search terms; and generating, using the large language model, the search query based on the one or more search terms. 14. The method of claim 11, wherein the large language model is trained on a dataset comprising at least one of regulatory requirements, industry standards, or legislative directives. 15. The method of claim 11, further comprising: 

 identifying, responsive to comparing the product metadata of the one or more products to compliance thresholds defined by the updates to the control parameters, that the one or more products fail to comply with the updates to the control parameters. 16. The method of claim 11, wherein the product metadata includes information related to product configurations, operational settings, usage patterns, or deployment environments. 17. The method of claim 11, further comprising: determining the code segments within the source code responsive to mapping the control parameters to the code segments implementing functionalities related to the control parameters. 18. The method of claim 11, further comprising: executing the large language model, trained on a dataset of control parameters, to determine the code segments within the source code. 19. The method of claim 11 , further comprising: generating code modifications to the code segments based on the updates to the control parameters; and executing the generated code modifications to comply with the updates to the control parameters. 20. A non-transitory computer readable medium including one or more instructions stored thereon and executable by a processor to: filter using a large language model, data related to control parameters used by one or more products, the control parameters indicated via a chatbot interface; detect, using the large language model, updates to the control parameters based on the filtered data; identify, based on the updates to the control parameters and product metadata, that the one or more products fail to comply with the updates to the control parameters; 

 determine code segments within a source code corresponding to the one or more products, the code segments related to the control parameters; and modify the code segments to comply with the updates to the control parameters.
